# agent-gorgon

3-layer hook defense for Claude Code that stops AI agents from fabricating tool
output when a registered tool exists for the task.

## What it is

agent-gorgon intercepts three points in the Claude Code agent loop:

| Hook | When | What |
|---|---|---|
| UserPromptSubmit | Before model thinks | Injects registered tool entry commands into context |
| Stop | After model finishes | Detects fabricated structured output when tool was not called |
| PreToolUse (Bash) | Before bash runs | Blocks ad-hoc tool reimplementations |

Plus a discovery engine — `find_tool.py` — that reads YAML manifests and
returns copy-paste entry commands for natural-language queries.

## When to use

- You have Claude Code agents with registered tools that sometimes fabricate output
- You need an auditable trail: tool-invoked vs model-generated
- You want defense-in-depth without a policy-engine project

## When not to use

- Agent runtimes without pre/post hook points
- Single-shot chat with no registered tools
- Process sandboxing or network egress control (different layer)

## Install

```bash
git clone https://github.com/hermes-labs-ai/agent-gorgon
cd agent-gorgon
bash install.sh
```

Zero runtime dependencies. Python 3.10+.

## Key commands

```bash
# Find a registered tool for a task
python3 find_tool.py "score EU AI Act compliance"

# Run the test suite
python3 -m pytest tests/ -q
# 105 tests, all hooks + discovery engine
```

## Add your own tool

1. Copy `manifests/_template.yaml` to `manifests/<tool-name>.yaml`
2. Fill `name`, `path`, `entry`, `description`, `version`, `input.example`, `output.example_output`
3. Run `python3 find_tool.py "<tool name>"` to confirm it appears

## Source

https://github.com/hermes-labs-ai/agent-gorgon
MIT. Part of the Hermes Labs audit infrastructure stack.


## About Hermes Labs

Hermes Labs is an independent AI-reliability lab building open-source tools that catch silent failure modes in production AI. More at https://hermes-labs.ai
